integrity.caveat: SEED_SPECS in schemas/seed_data.py informed synthetic case authoring; final labels in data/labels/labels.json were human-confirmed separately. Agent prompts and planner logic do not read labels at runtime.

case_results.drafted_email:
To Whom It May Concern:

This is a prior authorization request for adalimumab (Humira) for the treatment of rheumatoid arthritis.

Patient: Avery Chen, Age 61
Case ID: case-002
Diagnosis: Rheumatoid arthritis (ICD-10 M06.9), RF and anti-CCP positive

Clinical Summary:
- Disease duration: 22 months
- Failed conventional DMARDs: 3 documented trials
- Methotrexate trial: 20 weeks at stable dose
- Current DAS28 score: 5.1

This request meets all payer policy criteria:
✓ Confirmed RA diagnosis (ICD-10 M05/M06)
✓ Disease duration ≥6 months (22 months documented)
✓ Failed ≥2 conventional DMARDs (3 documented failures)
✓ DAS28 score ≥3.2 (current score 5.1)
✓ Methotrexate trial ≥12 weeks (20 weeks completed)

Please approve this prior authorization request at your earliest convenience.

Thank you,
Rheumatology Clinic
case_results.email_subject: Prior Authorization Request - Adalimumab (Humira) - Case-002 - Avery Chen

Dear Prior Authorization Department,

We are submitting a prior authorization request for adalimumab (Humira) for our patient Taylor Brooks (age 66) with rheumatoid arthritis.

Clinical Summary:
- Diagnosis: Rheumatoid arthritis (ICD-10 M06.9), RF and anti-CCP positive
- Disease duration: 30 months
- Failed conventional DMARDs: 4 documented trials
- DAS28 score: 5.6 (indicating high disease activity)
- Methotrexate trial: 24 weeks at stable dose

All payer criteria for adalimumab coverage are met:
✓ Confirmed RA diagnosis
✓ Disease duration ≥6 months (30 months)
✓ Failed ≥2 DMARDs (4 failed)
✓ DAS28 ≥3.2 (score: 5.6)
✓ Methotrexate trial ≥12 weeks (24 weeks)

We respectfully request approval for adalimumab therapy.

Thank you for your consideration.

Rheumatology Department

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Rheumatology Team,

We are processing a prior authorization request for adalimumab (Humira) for patient Quinn Patel (Case ID: case-008).

The patient meets most payer criteria for coverage, but we need clarification on one required field:

**Missing Information:**
- Duration of methotrexate trial in weeks (policy requires minimum 12 weeks)

The clinical note indicates the patient is "currently on methotrexate" but does not specify the duration of the trial.

**Current Status:**
- Diagnosis: Rheumatoid arthritis (confirmed, ICD-10 M06.9)
- Disease duration: 12 months ✓
- Failed DMARDs: 2 ✓
- DAS28 score: 4.1 ✓
- Methotrexate trial: Duration NOT specified

Please provide the duration of the methotrexate trial so we can complete this prior authorization review.

Thank you,
Prior Authorization Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Auth case-008: Additional Information Needed for Humira - Quinn Patel

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Provider,

The prior authorization request for adalimumab (Humira) for patient Drew Kim (Case ID: case-009) has been denied due to insufficient documentation of failed conventional DMARD trials.

Payer Policy Requirement: Failure of at least 2 conventional DMARDs
Documented: Only 1 failed DMARD (methotrexate, 13 weeks)

All other criteria are met:
- Confirmed RA diagnosis (ICD-10 M06.9, RF/anti-CCP positive) ✓
- Disease duration 8 months (≥6 months required) ✓
- DAS28 score 3.8 (≥3.2 required) ✓
- Methotrexate trial 13 weeks (≥12 weeks required) ✓

To resubmit this request, please provide documentation of a second failed conventional DMARD trial (e.g., sulfasalazine, hydroxychloroquine, leflunomide) with duration, dosage, and reason for discontinuation or inadequate response.

If additional DMARD trials were completed that were not documented in the submitted materials, please provide that information for reconsideration.

Thank you,
Prior Authorization Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Auth Denied - Case-009: Adalimumab for Drew Kim - Insufficient DMARD Trials

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Rheumatology Team,

We are processing a prior authorization request for adalimumab (Humira) for patient Skyler Reed (age 53, case-010).

The payer requires documentation of disease duration (minimum 6 months) for rheumatoid arthritis. This information was not found in the submitted clinical note.

All other criteria are met:
- Confirmed RA diagnosis (ICD-10 M06.9, RF and anti-CCP positive)
- Failed 2 conventional DMARDs
- DAS28 score: 4.2 (meets threshold of 3.2)
- Methotrexate trial: 16 weeks (meets 12-week minimum)

Please provide:

Once received, we can complete the prior authorization review.

Thank you,
Prior Auth Team

case_results.drafted_email:
Case ID: case-013
Patient: Robin Hale, age 48
Drug: Adalimumab (Humira)
Condition: Rheumatoid Arthritis

DECISION: DENY-RISK

This prior authorization does not meet payer policy requirements and is at high risk for denial.

REASON FOR DENIAL RISK:
The payer requires failure of at least 2 conventional DMARDs. Documentation shows only 1 failed DMARD trial.

CRITERIA REVIEW:
✓ Confirmed RA diagnosis (ICD-10 M06.9): Met
✓ Disease duration ≥ 6 months (12 months documented): Met
✗ Failed ≥ 2 conventional DMARDs (only 1 documented): NOT MET
✓ DAS28 score ≥ 3.2 (4.5 documented): Met
✓ Methotrexate trial ≥ 12 weeks (16 weeks documented): Met

RECOMMENDATION:
Before resubmitting, either:
1. Document an additional failed conventional DMARD trial, or
2. Initiate and document failure of a second conventional DMARD, or
3. Consider peer-to-peer review with the payer to discuss clinical justification for biologic therapy with only one failed DMARD

Please contact the prior-auth team if you have questions or additional clinical documentation.
case_results.email_subject: Prior-Auth Denial Risk - Case-013 - Robin Hale - Adalimumab

case_results.drafted_email:
Case ID: case-014
Patient: Jesse Park, age 51
Drug: Adalimumab (Humira)
Condition: Rheumatoid Arthritis

DECISION: DENY - Does not meet payer criteria

REASON: The patient does not meet the minimum disease duration requirement. The payer policy requires at least 6 months of documented rheumatoid arthritis, but the patient has only 4 months of disease duration.

CRITERIA MET:
✓ Confirmed RA diagnosis (ICD-10 M06.9, RF and anti-CCP positive)
✓ Failed 2 conventional DMARDs
✓ DAS28 score 4.0 (≥3.2 required)
✓ Methotrexate trial 14 weeks (≥12 weeks required)

CRITERIA NOT MET:
✗ Disease duration: 4 months (6 months required)

RECOMMENDATION: Resubmit prior authorization request after the patient reaches 6 months of documented rheumatoid arthritis disease duration (approximately 2 months from now). All other criteria are currently met.

Please continue current treatment management and plan to resubmit in 2 months.
case_results.email_subject: Prior Auth Denied - Case-014: Adalimumab (Humira) for Jesse Park - Insufficient Disease Duration

case_results.drafted_email:
Case ID: case-015
Patient: Cameron Lee, age 60
Drug: Adalimumab (Humira)
Condition: Rheumatoid Arthritis

DENIAL RATIONALE:
The prior authorization request does not meet payer policy requirements due to the following:

1. DAS28 Score Insufficient: Current score is 2.9, below the required minimum of 3.2 for biologic therapy coverage.

2. Methotrexate Trial Duration Inadequate: Only 10 weeks documented, while policy requires at least 12 weeks at stable dose.

Met Criteria:
- Confirmed RA diagnosis (M06.9, RF and anti-CCP positive) ✓
- Disease duration 15 months (≥6 months required) ✓
- Failed 2 conventional DMARDs (≥2 required) ✓

RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Continue methotrexate for at least 2 more weeks to meet the 12-week minimum
- Reassess DAS28 score after completing adequate methotrexate trial
- Resubmit prior authorization if DAS28 remains ≥3.2 after completing requirements

Please contact the prior auth team with questions.
case_results.email_subject: Prior Auth Denial - Case-015 - Adalimumab for Cameron Lee

Dear Prior Authorization Team,

We are submitting a prior authorization request for semaglutide (Ozempic) for our patient Blair Tate (age 67, Case ID: case-022).

Diagnosis: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (ICD-10: E11.9)
Diabetes Duration: 12 years
Most Recent A1C: 8.5%
Metformin Trial: 9 months at maximum tolerated dose
BMI: 34.0

This request meets all payer policy requirements:
- Type 2 diabetes diagnosis confirmed
- A1C ≥7.0% (patient's A1C: 8.5%)
- Metformin trial ≥3 months at tolerated dose (patient: 9 months)

The patient has failed to achieve adequate glycemic control on metformin monotherapy and requires advancement to GLP-1 agonist therapy for diabetes management.

Please approve this medically necessary request.

Sincerely,
Endocrinology Department

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Provider,

The prior authorization request for semaglutide (Ozempic) for patient River Stone (Case ID: case-028) does not meet payer policy criteria and is recommended for denial.

Reason for denial:
- Patient's most recent A1C is 6.4%, which is below the payer's required threshold of at least 7.0% for semaglutide coverage.

Documented criteria:
✓ Type 2 diabetes diagnosis confirmed
✓ Metformin trial: 8 months (exceeds 3-month requirement)
✓ BMI: 31.0 (documented)
✗ A1C: 6.4% (below 7.0% threshold)

The patient has adequate diabetes control with current therapy. If clinical circumstances change and A1C rises to ≥7.0%, a new prior authorization request may be submitted.

Please contact our office if you have questions or wish to discuss alternative options.

Best regards,
Prior Authorization Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Auth Denial - Case-028: Semaglutide (Ozempic) for River Stone

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Provider,

The prior authorization request for semaglutide (Ozempic) for patient Sloane Hart (Case ID: case-029) does not meet payer coverage criteria at this time.

Denial Reason:
The payer requires a minimum 3-month trial of metformin at tolerated dose. Current documentation shows only 1 month of metformin trial.

Clinical Summary:
- A1C: 7.8% (meets ≥7.0% requirement)
- BMI: 33.5 (documented)
- Diabetes duration: 9 years
- Metformin trial: 1 month (requires 3 months minimum)

Next Steps:
The patient should continue metformin therapy for at least 2 additional months. A new prior authorization request can be submitted once the 3-month metformin trial requirement is met, assuming continued inadequate glycemic control.

Please contact our office with any questions.

Best regards,
Prior Authorization Review Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Auth Denied - Case-029: Semaglutide (Ozempic) for Sloane Hart

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Provider,

The prior authorization request for semaglutide (Ozempic) for patient Winter Lane (age 64, case-031) does not currently meet payer policy requirements.

Reason for denial:
The patient's metformin trial duration is 2 months, which does not meet the minimum requirement of at least 3 months at tolerated dose.

Criteria met:
- Type 2 diabetes diagnosis confirmed (11 years duration)
- A1C of 7.1% (meets ≥7.0% threshold)
- BMI documented at 30.5

Next steps:
Please continue the patient on metformin for at least one additional month (total 3 months), then resubmit the prior authorization request with updated documentation.

If there are clinical contraindications to continuing metformin or if the patient is truly intolerant at any dose, please provide documentation for consideration of an exception.

Best regards,
Prior Authorization Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Authorization Denial - Semaglutide (Ozempic) for Winter Lane (Case-031)

case_results.drafted_email:
Re: Prior Authorization Request for erenumab (Aimovig)
Case ID: case-041
Patient: Ella Moss, age 52
Diagnosis: Chronic migraine (G43.7)

The prior authorization request is currently PENDING additional documentation.

The following information has been verified:
- Chronic migraine diagnosis: Confirmed
- Migraine days per month: 16 (meets ≥15 requirement)
- Failed triptan trials: 2 documented (meets ≥2 requirement)

MISSING REQUIRED INFORMATION:
The payer policy requires documentation of failure of at least 1 preventive migraine medication. The current clinical note does not document any preventive medication trials.

Please provide:
1. Specific preventive medication(s) tried (e.g., propranolol, topiramate, amitriptyline, valproate)
2. Duration of each trial
3. Reason for discontinuation or failure (lack of efficacy, intolerance, contraindication)

Once this documentation is received, we can resubmit the prior authorization request.

Thank you.
case_results.email_subject: Additional Information Needed - Prior Auth Case case-041 (Ella Moss - Aimovig)

case_results.drafted_email:
Case ID: case-042
Patient: Zoe Hart, age 44
Drug: erenumab (Aimovig)
Condition: chronic migraine

DECISION: DENY - Does not meet payer criteria

REASON:
The patient does not meet the payer's minimum trial requirements:

- Chronic migraine diagnosis (G43.7): ✓ MET
- Migraine days per month ≥15: ✓ MET (18 days documented)
- Failed at least 2 triptans: ✗ NOT MET (only 1 documented: sumatriptan)
- Failed at least 1 preventive medication: ✗ NOT MET (0 preventive trials documented)

RECOMMENDATION:
To resubmit for coverage, please document:
1. Trial and failure of at least one additional triptan medication
2. Trial and failure of at least one preventive migraine medication (e.g., beta-blockers, anticonvulsants, or antidepressants)

Please contact our office if you have questions or wish to discuss alternative coverage pathways.
case_results.email_subject: Prior Auth DENIED - Case-042: Aimovig for Zoe Hart

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Neurology Team,

The prior authorization request for erenumab (Aimovig) for patient Greta Lowe (Case ID: case-045) has been denied based on payer policy criteria.

Denial Reason:
The payer policy requires a documented chronic migraine diagnosis. The clinical documentation indicates 'episodic migraine without chronic migraine features,' which does not meet this mandatory criterion.

Patient Details:
- Age: 48
- Migraine days per month: 16
- Failed triptan trials: 2
- Failed preventive medication trials: 1

While the patient meets the frequency threshold (≥15 migraine days/month) and medication trial requirements, the diagnosis classification does not align with payer criteria for erenumab coverage.

Next Steps:
If the clinical picture supports a chronic migraine diagnosis, please provide updated documentation. Otherwise, alternative treatment options should be considered.

Please contact the prior authorization team with any questions.

Best regards,
Prior Authorization Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Authorization Denial - Case-045 - Greta Lowe - Aimovig

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Neurology Team,

The prior authorization request for erenumab (Aimovig) for patient Hazel Voss (Case ID: case-046) has been denied based on insufficient triptan trial documentation.

Payer Policy Requirements:
- Chronic migraine diagnosis: ✓ Met (G43.7)
- At least 15 migraine days per month: ✓ Met (17 days)
- At least 2 failed triptan trials: ✗ NOT MET (only 1 documented)
- At least 1 failed preventive medication: ✓ Met (1 trial)

The patient has only 1 documented failed triptan trial, but the payer requires at least 2 failed triptan trials for coverage.

Next Steps:
If the patient has additional triptan trial history not documented in the current submission, please provide that information for resubmission. Otherwise, consider trialing an additional triptan medication before resubmitting this request.

Please contact the prior auth team if you have questions.

Best regards,
Prior Authorization Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Auth Denied - Case-046 - Aimovig for Hazel Voss

case_results.drafted_email:
Dear Provider,

The prior authorization request for erenumab (Aimovig) for patient Iris Boone (age 37, Case ID: case-047) has been denied based on payer policy criteria.

DENIAL REASON:
The patient does not meet the minimum migraine frequency threshold required by the payer policy. The policy requires at least 15 migraine days per month, but the patient currently experiences 14 migraine days per month.

CRITERIA MET:
- Chronic migraine diagnosis: Yes (G43.7)
- Failed triptans (minimum 2): Yes (2 documented failures)
- Failed preventive medication (minimum 1): Yes (1 documented failure)

CRITERIA NOT MET:
- Migraine days per month: 14 (requires ≥15)

NEXT STEPS:
If the patient's migraine frequency increases to meet the threshold of 15 or more days per month, please resubmit with updated documentation. Alternatively, you may consider appealing this decision with additional clinical justification if you believe the patient warrants an exception to policy criteria.

Please contact our office if you have questions or wish to discuss this case.

Sincerely,
Prior Authorization Review Team
case_results.email_subject: Prior Authorization Denied - Case-047: Erenumab (Aimovig) for Iris Boone
